news.t: Three ETFs Poised to Break Out Boosted by Tech, Global Trends
Putnam Investments Launches Emerging Markets ex-China ETF

holdings.infoBox: PEMX is an equity ETF with a total of 58 individual holdings. The top holdings are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company stock at 19.36%, Samsung Electronics at 11.75%, SK hynix at 5.09%, Putnam Government Money Market Fund Class P at 4.26%, and SK Square at 3.73%.

overview.description: The Putnam Emerging Markets ex-China ETF (PEMX) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund is an actively managed fund that pursues alpha over a full market cycle through fundamental research in emerging markets, excluding China. The fund targets a portfolio comprised of value and/or growth stocks. PEMX was launched on May 17, 2023 and is issued by Putnam.

data.title: Active ETFs are Over 30% of $420B Net Inflows in the U.S.
data.text: Active ETFs are a little over 30% of the $420B of net inflows in the U.S., notes David Mann. He discusses the state of the ETF landscape.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: May be short-term pain but long-term opportunity in emerging markets
